data.task_instructions: Read the loaded paper and extract exactly one chemical or experimental-condition candidate: estradiol treatment in embryonic zebrafish segmentation experiments. Preserve dose/timing/context and one record_evidence span-backed evidence record. Do not resolve ChEBI or condition ontology IDs in the extractor.

evidence_preview.verified_quote: g Standard long-term small molecule treatment with 50 μM estradiol caused strong developmental defects with rudimentary head and axis in wild type embryos (arrowheads). h Pulse experiments of 50 μM estradiol from 10 hpf for four hours showed recovered head and axis structures (arrowheads) and specific myotome boundary disruptions of up to 13 segments (bracket).

evidence_records.verified_quote: g Standard long-term small molecule treatment with 50 μM estradiol caused strong developmental defects with rudimentary head and axis in wild type embryos (arrowheads). h Pulse experiments of 50 μM estradiol from 10 hpf for four hours showed recovered head and axis structures (arrowheads) and specific myotome boundary disruptions of up to 13 segments (bracket).

run_finished.response:
The extraction from the document identified the following chemical treatment in embryonic zebrafish segmentation experiments:

- **Chemical**: Estradiol
- **Dose**: 50 µM
- **Timing**: From 10 hours post-fertilization (hpf) for four hours
- **Context**: Standard long-term small molecule treatment
- **Verified Quote**: "Standard long-term small molecule treatment with 50 µM estradiol caused strong developmental defects with rudimentary head and axis in wild type embryos (arrowheads). Pulse experiments of 50 µM estradiol from 10 hpf for four hours showed recovered head and axis structures (arrowheads) and specific myotome boundary disruptions of up to 13 segments (bracket)." (Page 5, Results section, Fig. 4)

This information was successfully extracted and validated from the document.

trial.title: Small molecule screen in embryonic zebrafish using modular variations to target segmentation
